title: Accessibility ID & UiAutomator Selectors
overview: Mobile-specific locator strategies: Accessibility ID is the preferred cross-platform locator. UiAutomator2 selectors provide Android-specific advanced querying.
whyItMatters: Mobile locators differ from web locators. Using native locators (Accessibility ID, UiAutomator) drastically improves test performance and reliability compared to XPath.
explanation: Accessibility ID maps to `content-description` on Android and `accessibilityIdentifier` on iOS, making it ideal for cross-platform tests. When you need complex queries on Android, `UiAutomator2` provides native Java-based selectors like `new UiSelector().text("Submit")` which execute much faster than XPath. On iOS, you can use `-ios class chain` or `-ios predicate string` for similar performance benefits. XPath should be the last resort due to XML tree traversal overhead.
keyPoints: Accessibility ID is the fastest and most robust cross-platform locator.
UiAutomator2 uses Android's native UIAutomator API for fast element finding.
UiScrollable is used for scrolling to elements on Android.
XPath is significantly slower on mobile than on web.
realWorldExample: In an e-commerce app, a list of products might require scrolling. You use `UiScrollable` via `androidUIAutomator` to automatically scroll until the specific product text is visible, rather than writing a custom scroll loop.
commonMistakes: Using XPath for everything, causing slow test execution.
Confusing `resource-id` with `Accessibility ID` (resource-id should be located using `AppiumBy.id`).
bestPractices: Request developers to add accessibility identifiers to all interactable UI elements.
Use `-ios class chain` instead of XPath for iOS for better performance.
Use `androidUIAutomator` for complex text matching and scrolling on Android.

interviewQuestions.q: Why is Accessibility ID preferred over XPath in Appium?
interviewQuestions.a: Accessibility ID is evaluated natively by the OS (UIAutomator/XCUITest), making it extremely fast. XPath requires Appium to dump the entire UI hierarchy to XML and parse it, which is resource-intensive and slow.

revisionSummary: Accessibility ID is the best cross-platform locator. For Android-specific advanced search and scrolling, use `AppiumBy.androidUIAutomator` with `UiSelector` and `UiScrollable`. Avoid XPath to maintain performance.
